_comment: Source of truth for /grants/. 'deadlines' is an array of upcoming ISO dates; leave empty for rolling deadlines (the entry will appear under 'Rolling & annually recurring').

grants.name: NSF Graduate Research Fellowship Program
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; field-specific dates mid-October.
grants.description: Three-year fellowship for early-career graduate students in NSF-supported STEM disciplines, including computer & information sciences.

grants.name: Goldwater Scholarship
grants.deadlineNote: Campus deadlines run earlier; check your institutional rep.
grants.description: Undergraduate award for U.S. sophomores and juniors intending research careers in natural sciences, engineering, and mathematics.

grants.name: Knight-Hennessy Scholars
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; full funding for any Stanford graduate program.
grants.description: Graduate scholarship at Stanford open globally; supports doctoral and masters study across the sciences and beyond.

grants.name: Rhodes Scholarship
grants.deadlineNote: Country-specific deadlines vary; check your constituency.
grants.description: Postgraduate scholarship for study at the University of Oxford; multiple constituencies, including India.

grants.name: HFSP Postdoctoral Fellowships
grants.deadlineNote: LOI due May 12, 2026; Full Proposal due September 24, 2026. Long-Term and Cross-Disciplinary tracks.
grants.description: Postdoctoral fellowships for life-science research with an international move; cross-disciplinary track open to physicists, chemists, computer scientists, and engineers entering life sciences.

grants.name: EMBO Postdoctoral Fellowships
grants.deadlineNote: Two annual rounds: late January and mid-July (next: 10 July 2026).
grants.description: Two-year postdoctoral fellowships for molecular life sciences research in EMBC member states and partner countries.

grants.name: Marie Skłodowska-Curie Postdoctoral Fellowships
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; European and Global Fellowship streams.
grants.description: Mobility fellowships supporting postdoctoral researchers in any discipline, hosted in or moving to Europe.

grants.name: Schmidt Science Fellows
grants.funder: Schmidt Sciences (in partnership with Rhodes Trust)
grants.deadlineNote: Nominations from partner universities; check internal deadlines.
grants.description: Postdoctoral fellowship for early-career scientists pivoting into a new discipline to tackle interdisciplinary problems.

grants.name: Damon Runyon Postdoctoral Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Annual deadline mid-August (Aug 14, 2026).
grants.description: Four-year fellowship for theoretical and experimental approaches in cancer research, broadly defined.

grants.name: Sloan Research Fellowships
grants.deadlineNote: Nomination by department head; seven scientific fields. Nominations open July 15; close mid-September.
grants.description: Two-year fellowship for outstanding early-career faculty in seven scientific fields, including computer science and mathematics.

grants.name: Packard Fellowships for Science and Engineering
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; nominations from invited universities only.
grants.description: Unrestricted five-year award supporting early-career faculty in the natural and physical sciences and engineering.

grants.name: NSF CAREER Award
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; directorate-specific deadlines vary by a few days.
grants.description: Foundation-wide award for early-career faculty integrating research and education.

grants.name: ERC Starting Grant
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; for researchers 2–7 years post-PhD.
grants.description: European frontier-research grant for promising early-career investigators, open across all disciplines.

grants.name: ANRF Prime Minister Early Career Research Grant
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; windows announced on the ANRF portal.
grants.description: Five-year early-career grant for researchers in Indian institutions across the sciences.

grants.name: Royal Society University Research Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; for early-career researchers in the UK.
grants.description: Long fellowship for outstanding early-career scientists to establish independent research groups at UK or Republic of Ireland host institutions.

grants.name: HHMI Investigator Program
grants.deadlineNote: Periodic open competitions - watch HHMI announcements.
grants.description: Long-term investment in mid-career biomedical scientists; HHMI employs the investigator and supports the lab.

grants.name: DIA Research Translation Accelerator
grants.funder: Centre for Cellular and Molecular Platforms (C-CAMP)
grants.deadlineNote: PI must be a faculty member or scientific lead at an Indian institution; students/postdocs may co-apply.
grants.description: Accelerator for proof-of-concept innovations (TRL-3) in healthcare, agriculture, and environment, transforming them into scalable industry solutions.

grants.name: ANRF MAHA Water Programme
grants.funder: Anusandhan National Research Foundation & Ministry of Jal Shakti
grants.deadlineNote: Consortium-mode submission mandatory; closes 5:00 PM IST.
grants.description: Mission-driven research on India's water challenges - sustainable management, drinking-water access, climate resilience, and water quality.

grants.name: Aspirational Nutrient-Dense Food Products for Anaemia Prevention
grants.deadlineNote: Closes 11:30 PM IST; multidisciplinary collaborations encouraged.
grants.description: Prototype development of nutrient-dense food products targeting adolescent girls (10–19) and women of reproductive age (20–49) to reduce anaemia prevalence.

grants.name: Collaborative Scientific Research Programme (CSRP)
grants.funder: Indo-French Centre for the Promotion of Advanced Research (CEFIPRA)
grants.deadlineNote: Joint Indo-French submission; one PI from each country required.
grants.description: Indo-French collaborative research in mathematics, physics, chemistry, life sciences, materials science, environmental sciences, and biotechnology.

grants.name: Research Scholars Program - HIV / Viral Hepatitis
grants.deadlineNote: Open to early-career investigators from low- and middle-income countries.
grants.description: Supports independent early-career investigators in HIV and viral hepatitis research aligned with sustainable development goals.

grants.name: Global Health Research - Tackling AMR in Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia
grants.funder: National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR)
grants.deadlineNote: Lead applicant from eligible LMIC institution; joint UK leads required.
grants.description: Bacterial and fungal AMR research on WHO priority lists, addressing real-world gaps in stewardship, infection prevention, and AMR-related health outcomes.

grants.name: Rowland Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: PhD completing or received after 1 May 2025; no human-subjects or clinical research.
grants.description: Establishes independent experimental research programmes in any science or engineering field at Harvard's Rowland Institute, Cambridge, MA.

grants.name: U.S.–India TRUST Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Closes 11:59 PM US ET; preference for U.S. citizens with no prior India experience.
grants.description: U.S.-based researchers spend a year at IIT Madras, IISc, or MAHE working on AI, quantum computing, semiconductors, biotechnology, defence, energy, or space.

grants.name: OpenAI Economic Research Exchange
grants.deadlineNote: RFP; publication track record in the proposed area required.
grants.amount: USD 25,000 PI grant + USD 7,500/mo RA stipend
grants.description: Funds empirical research on the economic impacts of AI using privacy-preserving analysis of OpenAI product and usage data - labour markets, education, household welfare, market structure, knowledge production.

grants.name: MultiObs Postdoctoral Fellowships
grants.deadlineNote: Eight positions across 5 workplans; PhD <7 years.
grants.description: Postdoc positions at UNICAMP across Living Lab, Data Infrastructure, Diversity & Funding Dynamics, Technology & Innovation, and STI Diplomacy workplans.

grants.name: Sydney Brenner Research Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; open to scholars, journalists, and writers in history of the life sciences.
grants.description: Supports research in the history of the life sciences using CSHL's Carnegie Library collections and archival staff.

grants.name: Hubert H. Humphrey Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Closes 11:59 PM IST; mid-career professionals with ≥5 years' experience.
grants.description: Non-degree academic + professional placement for mid-career leaders in policy fields including natural-resources, public-health, and technology policy.

grants.name: NIF Book Fellowships
grants.deadlineNote: Exact date in November TBD; check funder site. Indian citizens; non-fiction on contemporary India.
grants.description: Supports research and writing of original non-fiction books on contemporary India; stipend, editorial support, and publication with leading houses.

grants.name: Hanse-Wissenschaftskolleg Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Junior (PhD <5y) and Regular (PhD ≥5y) tracks; residential.
grants.description: Residential fellowship at HWK Delmenhorst; science streams include Brain & Mind, Earth, and Technology & Science.

grants.name: Leakey Foundation Research Grants
grants.deadlineNote: Two annual cycles: spring deadline Jan 10 (decisions mid-May) and fall deadline Jul 15 (decisions mid-December). Open to PhD candidates (ABD) and post-PhD researchers.
grants.description: Funds research on human origins - paleoanthropology (Miocene/Pliocene/Pleistocene), primate evolution and behaviour, and modern hunter-gatherer groups.

grants.name: LOTUS Programme - India–Japan Joint Research
grants.deadlineNote: Annual call; FY2026 cycle closed 9 Jun 2026. Watch JST for FY2027 announcement.
grants.description: Indian Master's, PhD, and postdoctoral researchers for joint research stays in Japan across AI/robotics, biotechnology, energy, materials, quantum, semiconductors, and networks/telecom.

grants.name: Tata Transformation Prize
grants.funder: Tata Group & New York Academy of Sciences
grants.deadlineNote: Annual prize; 2026 cycle closed 29 May. Watch NYAS for the 2027 call.
grants.description: Recognises India-based scientists and engineers developing transformational technologies in food security, sustainability, or healthcare.

grants.name: Advanced Research Grant (ARG)
grants.deadlineNote: Annual call; 2026 pre-proposals closed June 10, 2026. Next call expected April-May 2027. ARG subsumes the erstwhile SERB Core Research Grant (CRG).
grants.description: Restructured successor to the SERB Core Research Grant (CRG). Supports high-impact fundamental (TRL 1-3) and applied (TRL 1-6) research in frontier areas of science and engineering at academic institutions, national labs, and DSIR-SIRO recognised organisations.

grants.name: ARG-MATRICS (Mathematical Research Impact-Centric Support)
grants.deadlineNote: Annual call under ARG umbrella; 2026 window closed June 10, 2026. Next call expected mid-2027.
grants.amount: INR 5 lakh/year + overheads (incl. up to INR 5 lakh international travel)
grants.description: Supports active researchers in mathematical sciences, theoretical sciences (all areas of science and engineering other than mathematical), and quantitative analytical work. Streamlined fixed-grant scheme for established mathematicians and theoretical scientists.

grants.name: J. C. Bose Grant (JBG)
grants.deadlineNote: Annual nominations cycle; 2026 window closed Feb 10, 2026. Next cycle expected Jan-Feb 2027.
grants.amount: INR 25 lakh/year (grant up to INR 26 lakh/year reported)
grants.description: Recognises active senior scientists and engineers in India (age 45-65) for outstanding performance and contributions. By nomination from Heads of Institutions/Universities or Presidents of Science Academies. Indian nationals or OCIs in senior positions at Indian institutions.

grants.name: Ramanujan Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Always open. Nominations received throughout the year (current cycle runs until 31 December 2026, 5:00 pm). Selection announced twice annually.
grants.description: For brilliant Indian/Indian-origin scientists and engineers (under age 40) currently abroad to take up scientific research positions in India. Year-round nominations through institutions; one of the ANRF schemes that remains open year-round.

grants.name: POWER Fellowship (Promoting Opportunities For Women in Exploratory Research)
grants.deadlineNote: Always open. Nominations accepted throughout the year via online portal; no last date. Selection made twice a year.
grants.description: Mitigates gender disparity in S&T research funding. Recognises and supports outstanding women scientists in active research in Indian academic institutions and R&D labs. One of the two ANRF schemes that remain open year-round.

grants.name: POWER Research Grant
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling/year-round applications via the ANRF portal; selections made on Program Advisory Committee recommendations. Level I (premier institutions) up to INR 60 lakh; Level II (state universities/private colleges) up to INR 30 lakh.
grants.amount: Level I up to INR 60 lakh; Level II up to INR 30 lakh
grants.description: Two-tier research grant for women scientists (Level I and Level II based on career stage). Supports independent research projects in science and engineering at Indian academic institutions and R&D labs.

grants.name: National Post Doctoral Fellowship (N-PDF)
grants.deadlineNote: 2026 window closed 17 Feb 2026. Next annual call expected Jan-Feb 2027.
grants.amount: INR 80,000/month (PhD awarded); INR 50,000/month (thesis submitted) + research grant
grants.description: Supports early-career researchers with a PhD to carry out independent postdoctoral research in Indian institutions. Upper age 35 (relaxations apply).

grants.name: International Travel Support (ITS)
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling, event-tied: applications accepted in a 60-90 day window before event start date. Not eligible if availed in past 3 years.
grants.amount: Economy airfare, airport tax, visa fees; young scientists also get registration up to INR 50,000
grants.description: Financial assistance for Indian researchers to present research at international scientific events (conferences, seminars, workshops) abroad, and for young scientists (under 35) to attend training programmes and schools.

grants.name: Visiting Advanced Joint Research (VAJRA) Faculty Scheme
grants.deadlineNote: Currently under review by ANRF and not accepting applications. When active, submissions are accepted year-round with twice-yearly evaluation (January and July).
grants.amount: USD 15,000 first month + USD 10,000 each subsequent month (pro-rata)
grants.description: Adjunct/visiting faculty assignments for overseas scientists (incl. NRI/PIO/OCI) to undertake collaborative research at Indian public-funded academic and research institutions.

grants.name: KARYASHALA - High-End Workshop (Accelerate Vigyan)
grants.deadlineNote: Applications invited twice a year: May (for winter workshops Nov-Jan) and November (for summer workshops May-Jul) via the Accelerate Vigyan portal.
grants.description: Supports high-end hands-on training workshops at IITs, IISc, IISERs, NITs, CSIR/ICAR/ICMR labs for PG and PhD students from universities, colleges, and newly established institutes. Workshop hosts apply; students apply to selected workshops.

grants.name: Teachers Associateship for Research Excellence (TARE)
grants.deadlineNote: Call-based; notified periodically via ANRF/SERB websites. No active 2026 call confirmed on the official page.
grants.amount: INR 60,000/year fellowship (in addition to regular salary) + INR 5 lakh/yr research grant (split 50/50 between host and parent institutions) + overheads
grants.description: Facilitates mobility of faculty in State Universities, colleges, and private academic institutions to carry out research at established public-funded institutions (IITs, IISc, IISERs, NITs, CSIR/ICAR/ICMR labs, Central Universities).

grants.name: MAHA Drones - Components and Sub-Assemblies R&I Program
grants.deadlineNote: Stage 1 pre-proposal call: 27 April - 26 May 2026 (closed). Stage 2 to follow for shortlisted consortia.
grants.amount: Up to INR 50 Crore per consortium over 3 years
grants.description: Joint with ICMR and Ministry of Earth Sciences. Strengthens India's drone ecosystem by promoting indigenous development of critical components (motors, ESCs, Li-ion cells, GNSS, secure radios, EO/IR cameras, LiDAR, SAR payloads, sensors) and post-quantum cryptography.

grants.name: MAHA AI for Science and Engineering (AI-SE)
grants.deadlineNote: Mission launched in 2026 in collaboration with MeitY and scientific departments. Check ANRF portal for active CFPs.
grants.amount: Up to INR 30 Crore for 3-year projects (up to INR 50 Crore in exceptional cases); GPU usage capped at 70% of budget via MeitY infrastructure
grants.description: Flagship MAHA initiative supporting AI-driven research across science and engineering, in collaboration with MeitY, MoES, DBT, and DRDO. Covers foundation models for scientific discovery and AI applications in physical and life sciences.

grants.name: Ramalingaswami Re-entry Fellowship
grants.funder: Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Government of India
grants.deadlineNote: Annual call typically opens late in the year; recent cycle closed Feb 2026.
grants.amount: Fellowship of INR 1.35 lakh/month + INR 8.5 lakh/yr research grant + HRA
grants.duration: 3 years (extendable by up to 2 more years after performance appraisal)
grants.description: Re-entry fellowship for Indian nationals working abroad in biotechnology and life sciences who wish to return and take up scientific research in India.

grants.name: BioCARe - Biotechnology Career Advancement and Re-orientation for Women Scientists
grants.funder: Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Government of India
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling / periodic calls via e-PROMIS portal.
grants.amount: Up to INR 60 lakh (Cat-I) or INR 40 lakh (Cat-II) including fellowship
grants.description: First extramural research grant for women scientists (up to age 55) in life sciences and biotechnology to (re)launch independent research careers.

grants.name: DBT/Wellcome Trust India Alliance Fellowships (Early Career / Intermediate / Senior)
grants.deadlineNote: 2026 cycle: Senior and Intermediate Fellowships open 2 Feb - 10 April 2026; Clinical and Public Health Research Fellowships close 23 March 2026.
grants.amount: Up to INR 3.6 Cr (ECF) / INR 4.5 Cr (IF) / Senior tier research funds + salary support
grants.description: Flagship biomedical fellowships supporting independent researchers in India across basic, clinical, public health and veterinary sciences; the principal non-government biomedical fellowship in India.

grants.name: INSPIRE Faculty Fellowship
grants.funder: Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India
grants.deadlineNote: Annual call, typically announced mid-year via the INSPIRE portal.
grants.amount: INR 1.25 lakh/month consolidated + INR 7 lakh/yr research grant
grants.description: Faculty-level fellowship for young researchers (<32 years at award) with PhD in basic/applied sciences, engineering, medicine, agriculture, providing assured opportunity to do independent research at any recognised Indian institution.

grants.name: Women Scientist Scheme-B (WOS-B)
grants.funder: Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling submissions through online-wosa portal under WISE-KIRAN.
grants.description: Supports women scientists with a career break to take up S&T-based projects addressing societal problems. Part of the WISE-KIRAN umbrella (which also includes WISE-PDF, WISE-PHD, and the separate WIDUSHI scheme for senior women scientists in interdisciplinary research).

grants.name: Biotechnology Ignition Grant (BIG)
grants.funder: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC), DBT
grants.deadlineNote: Two calls per year; 25th call announced in early 2026, calls typically open ~45 days.
grants.description: Flagship early-stage biotech startup grant for scientist entrepreneurs and incubatee companies to translate ideas to proof of concept.

grants.name: ICMR ANVESHAN Extramural Grants (Small / Intermediate / Follow-on)
grants.deadlineNote: Recurring annual calls via the e-PMS portal; recent SMALL call closed 16 March 2026.
grants.amount: Tiered (Small / Intermediate / Follow-on) - varies by call
grants.description: Investigator-initiated biomedical research grants across the ICMR ANVESHAN extramural family covering biomedical, clinical, public health and translational research.

grants.name: ISRO RESPOND - Sponsored Research Programme
grants.funder: Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), Department of Space
grants.deadlineNote: RESPOND Basket 2025 currently referenced on ISRO portal; cycles per ISRO centre (VSSC, SAC, URSC, NRSC, LPSC, IPRC, PRL, IIRS, NESAC, ISTRAC, HSFC). Check ISRO site for 2026 updates.
grants.description: Mission-oriented academic R&D in space technology, space science and space applications via collaboration with Indian universities and research institutes.

grants.name: DAE-BRNS Research Projects
grants.funder: Board of Research in Nuclear Sciences (BRNS), Department of Atomic Energy
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling/year-round submission via BRNS portal across thrust areas of physical, chemical, biological, engineering and material sciences.
grants.amount: Project-based; equipment, manpower and contingency support
grants.description: Extramural research grants for university/institute scientists in nuclear and allied sciences, engineering and technology of relevance to DAE.

grants.name: National Agricultural Science Fund (NASF)
grants.deadlineNote: Periodic calls for research pre-proposals via NASF portal.
grants.description: Supports basic, strategic and frontier-application research in agriculture (genomics, climate-resilient agriculture, nanotechnology, sensors, plant/animal sciences) through consortium-mode projects.

grants.name: MNRE Research, Development & Demonstration (RD&D) Programme
grants.funder: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE), Government of India
grants.deadlineNote: Project proposals invited on a rolling basis across solar PV/thermal, wind, hydrogen, fuel cells, storage, bioenergy and geothermal.
grants.amount: Up to 100% support for govt/non-profit; 50-70% for industry
grants.description: Funds R&D and demonstration projects in renewable energy technologies; also supports the National Renewable Energy Science Fellowship (NRESF) at INR 1.2 lakh/month plus research grant.

grants.name: PRITHVI - Earth Sciences Research Programme (ACROSS/O-SMART/PACER/SAGE)
grants.funder: Ministry of Earth Sciences (MoES), Government of India
grants.deadlineNote: PRITHVI Vigyan (PRITHVI) is the MoES overarching scheme (2021-2026) merging ACROSS, O-SMART, PACER, SAGE, and REACHOUT; calls under sub-schemes announced periodically. Scheme cycle ends 2026; renewal pending.
grants.amount: Rs 4,797 crore allocation (2021-26) across atmosphere, ocean, polar, seismology, and REACHOUT sub-schemes
grants.description: PRITHVI Vigyan umbrella scheme of MoES funding extramural and collaborative research across five sub-schemes: ACROSS (atmosphere/climate), O-SMART (ocean), PACER (polar/cryosphere), SAGE (seismology/geosciences), and REACHOUT (research/training/outreach).

grants.name: Google Academic Research Awards
grants.deadlineNote: Two annual nomination cycles (spring and fall); successor to Research Scholar Program and Research Awards
grants.description: Unrestricted gifts and Google Cloud credits to faculty (especially early-career) for foundational and applied research in computing and technology. Consolidates the former Research Scholar Program and Faculty Research Awards.

grants.name: Google PhD Fellowship Program
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle; 2026 university nomination window closed (global deadline April 30, 2026). Next cycle expected spring 2027.
grants.amount: Region-dependent: US/Canada up to ~$85K/year (2 yrs); India up to $50K (4 yrs); Europe yearly bursary; SE Asia $10K/year (3 yrs); Africa/LatAm ~$15K/year (2-3 yrs)
grants.description: University-nominated PhD fellowship for outstanding graduate students in CS and related fields, including machine learning, systems, HCI, quantum, health research, and algorithms. Includes Google research mentor.

grants.name: Microsoft Research Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Proposals were due December 2, 2025; next cycle expected late 2026
grants.region: Global (Africa, Asia, Australia/NZ, Canada, Europe, Latin America, US)
grants.description: Funded collaboration with Microsoft Research on open research challenges for PhD students, postdocs, and faculty. Replaces the earlier MSR PhD Fellowship and Research Fellows India programs as a unified global program.

grants.name: Accelerating Foundation Models Research (AFMR)
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling/periodic calls for proposals across themes (Model Advancement, Cognition and Societal Benefits, AI for Science, etc.)
grants.amount: Azure AI compute credits + access to frontier models
grants.description: Grant program providing academic researchers access to state-of-the-art foundation models on Azure AI services, plus compute credits, to advance safety, robustness, and applications of AI.

grants.name: Amazon Research Awards (ARA)
grants.deadlineNote: Twice-yearly calls; Spring 2026 call closed May 13, 2026. Next call expected fall 2026.
grants.description: Funds academic researchers across themes including Agentic AI, AI for Information Security, Robotics, Build on Trainium (kernels and post-training), Amazon 2030, and Amazon Security. Awardees get an Amazon scientific contact and training resources.

grants.name: AWS Cloud Credit for Research
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling submissions; reviews on 90-120 day cycles
grants.amount: Up to $5,000 students; uncapped for faculty/staff
grants.description: AWS promotional credits for finite research projects to build cloud-hosted services, develop repeatable solutions, or migrate research processes/open data to the cloud.

grants.name: Meta Research PhD Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Annual fall cycle; 2026 applications open Aug 3, 2026 with Sep 20, 2026 deadline (reference letters Oct 6, 2026).
grants.description: Supports PhD students across AR/VR, AI, HCI, privacy, networking, programming languages, statistics, and related areas aligned with Meta's research. Includes travel funding for a research summit.

grants.name: NVIDIA Graduate Fellowship Program
grants.deadlineNote: Annual fall cycle (typical mid-September deadline). 2026-27 cycle closed Sep 15, 2025; next call expected Aug 2026 for 2027-28.
grants.description: Fellowship for PhD students who have completed at least their first year, focused on GPU computing, AI/ML, graphics, robotics, autonomous vehicles, HPC, and related areas. Requires a mandatory in-person NVIDIA Research internship.

grants.name: NVIDIA Academic Grant Program (Hardware/Compute)
grants.deadlineNote: Program currently not accepting new applications as of 2026; check page for future submission windows.
grants.amount: Up to 30,000 H100 80GB GPU-hours, RTX PRO 6000 GPUs, or DGX Spark systems
grants.description: Cloud GPU hours, hardware donations, and software for university faculty researching scientific simulation, quantum computing, physics-informed ML, AI training, and AI inference/agents. Proposals must incorporate NVIDIA models or software.

grants.name: Adobe Research Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: Annual fall nomination cycle (typically Oct-Nov deadline)
grants.description: Recognizes outstanding graduate students conducting research in AI/ML, computer vision, NLP, HCI, graphics, audio, and document intelligence. Includes salaried Adobe Research internship and an Adobe mentor.

grants.name: IBM PhD Fellowship Awards
grants.deadlineNote: By-invitation nominations; recent cycle covered Fall 2025/Spring 2026 with awards finalized Q4 2025; next nomination window expected fall 2026.
grants.amount: US universities: $40,000; other countries: $6,000-$25,000 (varies by country)
grants.duration: Fall/Spring academic year (renewable in some cases)
grants.description: Faculty-nominated PhD fellowship supporting students working on hybrid cloud, quantum computing, AI, open source, security/cybersecurity, data science, and systems. Students must have two years remaining at nomination.

grants.name: Apple Scholars in AI/ML PhD Fellowship
grants.deadlineNote: University-nominated; institutional nomination deadlines typically July-August for Fall start; 2026 cohort already announced
grants.amount: Tuition and fees + up to $40,000 annual stipend
grants.description: University-nominated fellowship for PhD students in their last 2-3 years of study. Areas include privacy-preserving ML, human-centered AI, ML theory, computer vision, speech/NLP, interactive ML and agents, and data-centric AI. Includes Apple mentor and internship opportunity.

grants.name: OpenAI Researcher Access Program
grants.deadlineNote: Reviewed quarterly (March, June, September, December)
grants.amount: Up to $1,000 in OpenAI API credits
grants.description: Subsidized OpenAI API access for academic and nonprofit researchers studying responsible AI deployment, risk mitigation, alignment, and societal impacts of AI systems.

grants.name: Anthropic Fellows Program
grants.deadlineNote: Two 2026 cohorts beginning May 2026 and July 2026; not strictly rolling
grants.description: Mentored AI-safety research program producing public outputs in collaboration with Anthropic researchers. Project areas: scalable oversight, adversarial robustness/AI control, mechanistic interpretability, AI security, model organisms, model welfare.

grants.name: NIH R01 Research Project Grant
grants.deadlineNote: Standard new R01 due dates: Feb 5, Jun 5, Oct 5; renewals/resubmissions Mar 5, Jul 5, Nov 5 - recurring annually.
grants.description: Flagship NIH investigator-initiated research project grant supporting discrete, specified, circumscribed biomedical research projects.

grants.name: NIH R21 Exploratory/Developmental Research Grant
grants.deadlineNote: Standard due dates: Feb 16, Jun 16, Oct 16 - recurring annually.
grants.amount: Up to $275k direct costs over two years
grants.description: Supports exploratory/developmental, high-risk biomedical research with novel approaches; no preliminary data required.

grants.name: NIH Director's Pioneer Award (DP1)
grants.deadlineNote: 2026 cycle applications due September 9, 2026; recurring annually.
grants.description: High-Risk, High-Reward award for exceptionally creative scientists pursuing pioneering biomedical, behavioral or social science approaches.

grants.name: NIH Director's New Innovator Award (DP2)
grants.deadlineNote: 2026 cycle applications due August 19, 2026; recurring annually.
grants.amount: $475k direct costs per year (up to ~$2.375M total over 5 years)
grants.description: Supports early-career investigators (within 10 years of terminal degree) proposing highly innovative, high-impact biomedical research.

grants.name: NIH Director's Early Independence Award (DP5)
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle; typically January submission.
grants.description: Allows exceptional junior scientists to skip the postdoc and move directly into an independent research position.

grants.name: NSF Expeditions in Computing
grants.deadlineNote: Preliminary proposals due June 22, 2026; full proposals March 31, 2027.
grants.description: Large, ambitious, multi-institutional research projects defining the future of computing and information science.

grants.name: NSF National AI Research Institutes
grants.deadlineNote: Recurring solicitations on rotating AI themes.
grants.description: Multi-institutional research centers advancing fundamental and use-inspired AI research across science and engineering.

grants.name: DOE Office of Science Early Career Research Program
grants.funder: US Department of Energy, Office of Science
grants.deadlineNote: 2026 cycle: pre-applications were due March 24, 2026; full applications June 2, 2026; recurring annually.
grants.description: Supports outstanding scientists in DOE-relevant fields (energy, physical/computational sciences) early in their careers.

grants.name: AFOSR Young Investigator Program (YIP)
grants.funder: Air Force Office of Scientific Research
grants.deadlineNote: FY2026 cycle deadlines (white papers July 25, 2025; full proposals September 19, 2025) have passed; FY2027 solicitation expected mid-2026 with similar annual cadence
grants.description: Funds early-career scientists/engineers (PhD within 7 years) conducting basic research of interest to the Air Force and Space Force.

grants.name: ONR Young Investigator Program (YIP)
grants.deadlineNote: FY2026 proposals were due August 1, 2025 (cycle closed); FY2027 solicitation expected to release mid-2026 with similar annual cadence
grants.description: Supports early-career academic researchers pursuing basic research of interest to the Navy and Marine Corps.

grants.name: ARO Early Career Program
grants.deadlineNote: No fixed deadline; coordinate with program manager.
grants.description: Funds early-career university faculty for basic research aligned with Army priorities; submitted via core program managers.

grants.name: Simons Collaborations in Mathematics and the Physical Sciences
grants.deadlineNote: Annual LOI cycle; full proposals (by invitation) typically due February.
grants.description: Funds large team-science collaborations on fundamental questions in math, theoretical physics, and TCS.

grants.name: Moore Foundation Experimental Physics Investigators / EPiQS Initiative
grants.deadlineNote: EPiQS and EPI run periodic investigator competitions; 2026 competitions active.
grants.description: Supports experimental physicists and quantum materials researchers pursuing high-risk, foundational research.

grants.name: Beckman Young Investigator Program
grants.deadlineNote: 2027 cycle applications open August 2026.
grants.description: Supports promising tenure-track faculty in early stages of independent careers in chemical and life sciences with innovative methodology.

grants.name: Searle Scholars Program
grants.deadlineNote: 2027 cycle: institutional nominations due September 30, 2026.
grants.description: Supports young US tenure-track faculty in chemistry and biological sciences pursuing high-impact, innovative research.

grants.name: Pew Scholars Program in the Biomedical Sciences
grants.deadlineNote: 2027 cycle: nominations due May 13, 2026; applications September 3, 2026.
grants.description: Funds early-career biomedical researchers (within 3 years of first independent appointment) on a path to becoming leaders in their fields.

grants.name: ERC Consolidator Grant
grants.deadlineNote: Annual call; 2026 call closed Jan 13, 2026; next call typically opens autumn 2026.
grants.description: Supports excellent researchers 7-12 years post-PhD consolidating their independent research team in any field.

grants.name: ERC Advanced Grant
grants.deadlineNote: 2026 call opens May 28, 2026; deadline August 27, 2026.
grants.description: Funds established, world-class research leaders pursuing ground-breaking, high-risk projects in any field.

grants.name: Wellcome Early-Career Awards
grants.deadlineNote: Next deadline 21 July 2026.
grants.amount: Salary + up to GBP 400k research costs
grants.description: Supports early-career researchers (any discipline) developing their research identity and transitioning to independence in health-related science.

grants.name: Wellcome Career Development Awards
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle; next round opens later in 2026.
grants.description: Long-term funding for mid-career researchers leading ambitious, innovative research programmes in health-related science.

grants.name: Wellcome Discovery Awards
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle.
grants.description: Funds established researchers and teams pursuing bold, creative research with potential to transform understanding of health-related science.

grants.name: Allen Distinguished Investigators
grants.funder: Paul G. Allen Family Foundation / Allen Institute Frontiers Group
grants.deadlineNote: Topic-based RFAs released periodically through the year.
grants.description: Backs early-stage, high-risk research projects in biology and bioscience with potential to reinvent entire fields.

grants.name: Kavli Scholars (Bridge Funding)
grants.deadlineNote: Nominations only (by Kavli-affiliated individuals); no public rolling application.
grants.description: Bridge funding for scientists in astrophysics, nanoscience, neuroscience, or theoretical physics facing unexpected research disruption.

grants.name: CIFAR Azrieli Global Scholars Program
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle; next cycle expected fall 2026.
grants.description: Supports early-career researchers (within 5 years of first PI position) joining a CIFAR research program for interdisciplinary collaboration.

grants.name: ACM India–IARCS Travel Grants
grants.funder: Indian Association for Research in Computing Science / ACM India
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling; applicants typically notified within one month of submission. No support for instructional schools/internships; not twice within 12 months.
grants.description: Partial support to travel to international conferences in computing science to present original research. For students and faculty at Indian institutions; preference for graduate students (Integrated Masters, Integrated PhD, Masters, PhD); faculty at well-funded institutes typically do not qualify.

grants.name: SIGPLAN Professional Activities Committee (PAC) Travel Grant
grants.deadlineNote: Submit at least four weeks before the conference's early registration and hotel deadlines; multiple categories (students, professional members, scholars from marginalized groups, parents, international members).
grants.description: Funding to attend SIGPLAN-sponsored conferences (PLDI, POPL, OOPSLA, ICFP, PPoPP, etc.), including long-distance travel support from outside North America and Europe, and child-care/family support. Open to students and non-students; SIGPLAN membership not required.

grants.name: ACM SIGCOMM Student & GeoDiversity Travel Grants
grants.deadlineNote: SIGCOMM 2026 deadline 15 Jun 2026; notifications by 10 Jul 2026. Includes NSF (US-based), GeoDiversity, and N2Women tracks.
grants.description: Travel support for students and early-career researchers to attend ACM SIGCOMM 2026 (Denver, 17–21 Aug 2026). Multiple tracks including geographic diversity and women in networking.

grants.name: SIGMOD/PODS 2026 India Fellowships
grants.deadlineNote: 2026 application deadline (16 Mar 2026) has passed for this iteration; program recurs around SIGMOD/PODS host cycles.
grants.description: Fellowship supporting researchers (students, postdocs, faculty) based in India to attend SIGMOD/PODS 2026 (Bengaluru, 31 May – 5 Jun 2026). Covers part of the cost of attendance.

grants.name: SIGACT Student Travel Awards (STOC / FOCS)
grants.deadlineNote: STOC 2026 deadline 13 May 2026 (application + advisor letter); per-conference deadlines published on each STOC/FOCS travel-support page; advisor may support at most two students.
grants.description: Travel awards for students attending STOC and FOCS, covering registration, travel, and accommodation. NSF-funded slots restricted to students at US universities; SIGACT encourages applications from underrepresented groups.

grants.name: ACM Student Research Competition (SRC)
grants.funder: Association for Computing Machinery (sponsored by Microsoft Research)
grants.deadlineNote: Per-conference SRC deadlines; one SRC per student per program year (Apr 1 – Mar 31).
grants.description: International forum for undergraduate and graduate students to present original research at participating ACM conferences. Requires ACM student membership; advisors cannot be co-authors on the SRC entry. Grand Finals across all participating conferences each year.

grants.name: AAAI/SIGAI Doctoral Consortium
grants.deadlineNote: AAAI-26 DC cycle closed (application 30 Sep 2025; consortium 20-21 Jan 2026 in Singapore). AAAI-27 call expected late 2026.
grants.description: Doctoral consortium for current PhD students working in AI. Selected participants receive complimentary conference registration and may receive travel funding subject to availability. Open to PhD students worldwide.

grants.name: NeurIPS Financial Assistance
grants.deadlineNote: NeurIPS 2025 cycle closed 2 Oct 2025; NeurIPS 2026 (Sydney) financial assistance page typically opens a few months before the December conference.
grants.description: Need-based financial assistance for NeurIPS attendees, prioritising student and junior postdoc (<2 years) authors. Criteria include accessibility, affinity-group membership, financial hardship and first-time attendance.

grants.name: ICML Financial Aid
grants.deadlineNote: ICML 2026 application closed 18 May 2026; decisions on 1 Jun 2026. Award covers conference registration and 7-night hotel for in-person attendees; no travel support.
grants.description: ICML 2026 financial aid covers conference registration and a 7-night hotel stay for in-person attendees (virtual registration for virtual attendees); travel costs are not supported. Eligibility limited to applicants with an accepted paper linked to their ICML.cc email.

grants.name: ICLR Financial Assistance
grants.deadlineNote: ICLR 2026 application closed 1 Mar 2026; notifications sent 10 Mar 2026. Tier 1 prepay valued ~$1,745; reimbursement capped at $1,500.
grants.description: Supports registration fees, accommodation, and travel for ICLR contributors who would otherwise be unable to attend. Priority for student authors, underrepresented demographics, and underrepresented geographic regions. ~325 researchers supported at ICLR 2025.

grants.name: Heidelberg Laureate Forum (HLF) - Young Researchers
grants.deadlineNote: 13th HLF (13–18 Sep 2026) application phase closed 11 Feb 2026; 14th HLF call typically opens November 2026.
grants.description: Fully funded week-long forum bringing 200 selected young researchers in mathematics and computer science into cross-generational dialogue with Turing Award, ACM Prize in Computing, Abel Prize, Fields Medal, IMU Abacus Medal and Nevanlinna Prize laureates. Open to undergraduates, graduate students and recent PhDs.

grants.name: Lindau Nobel Laureate Meetings - Young Scientists
grants.funder: Council & Foundation Lindau Nobel Laureate Meetings
grants.deadlineNote: Annual cycle; nomination via Academic Partners or Open Application. Disciplines rotate (Physics, Chemistry, Physiology/Medicine, with Economics added every third year).
grants.description: Selective meeting bringing several hundred young scientists (undergraduates, PhD students, postdocs) into week-long exchange with Nobel Laureates. Two routes: Academic Partner nomination or open application via the Lindau website.

grants.name: IEEE Computer Society Richard E. Merwin Student Scholarship
grants.deadlineNote: Twice yearly: 30 April and 30 September.
grants.description: Recognises active student volunteers in IEEE Student Branches / Computer Society Student Branch Chapters in CS / ECE / computer engineering. Winners serve as IEEE CS Student Ambassadors for one year. Requires IEEE CS membership ≥3 months and GPA ≥2.5/4.0.

grants.name: EMBO Scientific Exchange Grants
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling - applications accepted throughout the year; submit at least 3 months before the planned visit; decision within ~4 months.
grants.amount: Travel and subsistence; varies by destination
grants.description: Funds short research exchanges (up to 3 months) between laboratories in eligible countries to transfer expertise, learn techniques, or access infrastructure. Replaced the EMBO Short-Term Fellowships.

grants.name: Janelia Visiting Scientist Program
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling - Janelia FAQ explicitly states 'Proposals are accepted on a rolling-basis'; review takes 4–8 weeks.
grants.amount: Supports travel, visas, on-campus housing, and scientific expenses
grants.description: Brings graduate students, postdocs, and senior investigators to Janelia to pursue interdisciplinary research alongside Janelia scientists, often leveraging Janelia-developed technologies.

grants.name: Coefficient Giving Scientific Research Pre-Proposals
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling - brief pre-proposals accepted any time at science@openphilanthropy.org; only fits get a response.
grants.description: Supports high-risk, high-impact, under-funded scientific research, with emphasis on transformative basic science, biosecurity/pandemic preparedness, and human health. Submit a 2-page outline, rough budget, and timeline.

grants.name: Google Cloud Research Credits
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling - applications accepted continuously; decisions in 6–8 weeks.
grants.amount: Up to $5,000 USD (faculty/postdocs); up to $1,000 USD (PhD students)
grants.description: Provides Google Cloud credits for academic research across any field, including compute, storage, data analysis, and AI/ML (GPU/TPU) workloads.

grants.name: Simons Foundation Targeted Grants in Mathematics and Physical Sciences
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling - LOI can be submitted at any time via the Simons Award Manager; decision within ~3 months.
grants.description: Supports high-risk theoretical mathematics, physics, and theoretical computer science projects of exceptional promise and scientific importance. LOI required first; full proposals by invitation.

grants.name: Hyperspace Innovation Hackathon 2026
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling 2026 edition; check official Hack2skill event page for current registration window
grants.amount: Up to INR 2,00,000 prize pool plus AWS credits, goodies, certificates
grants.description: Deep-tech hackathon for student innovators across themes including AI/ML, DroneTech, SpaceTech, Robotics, IoT, smart cities, and sustainability. Shortlisted teams build live on-campus with AWS-certified mentors, aerospace experts, and AI researchers; projects target autonomous drones, AI-driven security, real-time satellite intelligence, disaster-response systems, and next-gen robotics.

grants.name: INDIA RUNS Hackathon 2026 (Data & AI Track)
grants.deadlineNote: Registration closes 28 June 2026 (2026-06-28); virtual grand finale 22 July 2026
grants.amount: INR 50 Lakh+ total prize pool (INR 10 Lakh for Data & AI track)
grants.description: National open hackathon with a Data & AI track (Track 1) for developers, AI/ML engineers, data scientists, and LLM practitioners. Track 1 challenges participants to build an AI candidate ranking system using real production data. Open to students, developers, and professionals across India; free entry, individuals or teams.

grants.name: Smart India Hackathon (SIH) 2026
grants.funder: Ministry of Education, Government of India / AICTE
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; SIH 2025 cycle closed Sept 2025. Next edition (SIH 2026) expected to open registrations Aug-Sept 2026 on sih.gov.in
grants.amount: Rs 1 lakh per winning team per problem statement
grants.description: Nationwide initiative by the Ministry of Education engaging Higher Education Institution students to solve problem statements submitted by government ministries, PSUs, industries and NGOs. 16 themes including Smart Automation, MedTech/BioTech, Agriculture, Robotics, Blockchain & Cybersecurity, Smart Education, and Disaster Management.

grants.name: Google Solution Challenge 2026 - Build with AI (India)
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; 2026 India edition ran 6 March 2026 - late June 2026. Next edition expected early 2027
grants.description: Hackathon for university student-developers in India to build AI-powered solutions using Amazon Bedrock, Gemini, or Vertex AI. Open to college students 18+. Teams of 1-4 members. Free registration.

grants.name: Bharatiya Antariksh Hackathon (BAH) 2026
grants.deadlineNote: Registration closes 1 July 2026 (2026-07-01). Grand finale (30 hours) on 6-7 August 2026
grants.amount: Free participation; ISRO internship + II AC travel reimbursement for finalists
grants.description: Third edition of ISRO's national space-tech hackathon with 15 problem statements bridging space exploration and terrestrial resource management - AI-powered climate digital twin, Chandrayaan-2 radar analysis, solar flare forecasting from Aditya-L1 data, exoplanet identification, generative AI for cloud removal in satellite imagery. Teams of 3-4 from Indian institutions (UG/PG/PhD). ISRO internship consideration for winners.

grants.name: Nasscom AI Code Sarathi - Agentic AI Hackathon
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling phases in 2026; Explore phase began Mar-Apr 2026; innovations to be showcased at AI Confluence July 2026
grants.description: Free, industry-focused national hackathon to build and validate agent-based AI systems on real-world problem statements (autonomous workflows, intelligent agents). Part of AI Code Sarathi initiative to upskill 150,000 developers across corporate, startup and academic sectors. Three-phase structure: Explore, Engineer, Excel. Blockchain-backed certificates.

grants.name: Shaastra 2027 Hackathons (NeuroHack, Programming Contest, FedEx SMART)
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; Shaastra 2026 held 2-6 January 2026. Next edition (Shaastra 2027) expected early January 2027 with registrations opening Oct-Nov 2026
grants.description: IIT Madras' annual technical festival featuring 80+ events including NeuroHack (brain-computer interface hackathon using real EEG data), Shaastra Programming Contest, FedEx SMART Hackathon, E-Contest (esoteric programming languages), Robo GP, and Digital Address DPI Innovation Hackathon. 27th edition with student-managed ISO 9001:2015 organization.

grants.name: Techfest 2026 IIT Bombay (hackAI and others)
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; Techfest 2025 held 22-24 December 2025. Next edition (Techfest 2026) expected late December 2026
grants.description: Asia's largest science and technology festival at IIT Bombay. Hosts hackathons including hackAI (one-day hackathon using uAgents library with Fetch.ai), competitions, exhibitions, lectures and workshops on robotics, AI, and engineering.

grants.name: Kshitij 2027 - KDSH Data Science Hackathon
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; Kshitij 2026 held 16-18 January 2026 with KDSH 6th edition. Next edition (Kshitij 2027) expected mid-January 2027
grants.description: Asia's largest techno-management festival hosting multiple hackathons - Kharagpur Data Science Hackathon (KDSH), Overnite, Source Code, On-Block Web3 Hackathon, Tech Triad Hackathon, Scam Bust Challenge, Lexicognition AI, Build with Gemini, NLP Challenge. Problem statements in blockchain, AI, cybersecurity, data science.

grants.name: HackIIIT 2027 - OSDG Annual FOSS Hackathon
grants.funder: Open Source Developers' Group, IIIT Hyderabad
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; HackIIIT 2026 ran Jan 24-26, 2026 (per OSDG page). Next edition (HackIIIT 2027) expected Jan 2027; current URL is an archive of the 2026 edition.
grants.description: OSDG IIIT Hyderabad's annual 24-hour hackathon dedicated to building Free and Open-Source Software (FOSS) projects. Focuses on impactful open-source contributions.

grants.name: e-Yantra Robotics Competition (eYRC) 2026-27
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; eYRC 2025-26 finale held 27-28 March 2026. Next edition (eYRC 2026-27) registrations typically open Aug-Sept 2026
grants.description: India's biggest project-based learning robotics competition, MoE-funded outreach program hosted at IIT Bombay. Two-stage 6-7 month competition spanning robotics challenges and innovation challenge (eYIC). 1,000+ institutions participate; finale at IIT Bombay.

grants.name: HACK IITK 2027 - National Cybersecurity Hackathon
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; HACK IITK 2026 closed Jan 2026 with grand finale Apr 2026. Next edition (HACK IITK 2027) expected with registrations opening Nov-Dec 2026
grants.amount: Prize pool exceeding Rs 30 lakhs + AWS credits + certifications
grants.description: National cybersecurity hackathon by C3iHub IIT Kanpur on Critical Infrastructure Security. Open to students, innovators and professionals. Tracks: Capture the Flag (CTF) and Solution Track covering Network Security, Cryptography, Hardware Security, IoT and AI Security, Digital Forensics, and Cybercrime Prevention. Hybrid format with finale at IIT Kanpur.

grants.name: NASA International Space Apps Challenge 2026
grants.deadlineNote: Registration opens July 17, 2026; hackathon weekend Oct 4-5, 2026. Annual; expected late Sep 2026 deadline.
grants.amount: Global judging, no cash prize (in-kind awards)
grants.description: World's largest annual NASA hackathon. Teams use NASA open data to build scientific and engineering solutions to space and Earth-science challenges over a 48-hour weekend at hundreds of local sites worldwide.

grants.name: Major League Hacking (MLH) 2026 Season
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling. 2026 season runs July 1, 2026 - June 30, 2027 with 300+ member hackathons globally.
grants.description: Official student hackathon league powering 300+ weekend invention competitions per season in CS, AI, hardware, and data science for ~500,000 student developers worldwide.

grants.name: Kaggle Competitions (continuously running)
grants.deadlineNote: Rolling year-round. Examples in 2026: NeuroGolf 2026 (deadline Jul 15, 2026), ARC Prize 2026 / ARC-AGI-3 (deadline Nov 2, 2026), BirdCLEF+ 2026.
grants.amount: Varies per competition; ARC Prize 2026 pool ~$1M
grants.description: Continuously running data-science and ML competitions hosted on Kaggle covering vision, NLP, science, biology, and AGI benchmarks. Open to individuals and teams worldwide.

grants.name: MIT Reality Hack 2027
grants.funder: Reality Hack Inc. / MIT Media Lab community
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; 2026 main event held Jan 22, 2026 with hybrid Reality Hack at AWE 2026 (May 28 - Jun 15, 2026). Site teases 'See you in 2027!'; applications typically open early autumn.
grants.description: Five-day experiential-technology hackathon at MIT focused on XR, AR/VR, AI, spatial computing, and hardware prototyping. Open to developers, designers, researchers and professionals globally.

grants.name: HackMIT 2026
grants.deadlineNote: Apply by July 4, 2026, 11:59 PM ET; event Sep 19-20, 2026.
grants.description: Annual 24-hour undergraduate student hackathon at MIT covering AI/ML, systems, hardware, and applied CS. Travel reimbursement available.

grants.name: TreeHacks 2027 (Stanford)
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; 2026 edition (12th) ran Feb 13-15, 2026. Next edition expected February 2027; applications typically open in autumn.
grants.description: Stanford's flagship 36-hour collegiate hackathon for CS, AI, hardware, biotech, and quantum projects. Travel and lodging covered for accepted hackers.

grants.name: HackHarvard 2026
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; October 2026 edition (10th anniversary). Applications typically open in late summer with deadline in September.
grants.description: Free 36-hour undergraduate hackathon at Harvard for CS, AI, web, and applied technology projects. Open to college students globally; travel reimbursement for select hackers.

grants.name: MIT iQuHACK 2027 (Quantum Hackathon)
grants.funder: MIT iQuISE, IBM Quantum, IQM, IonQ, Quantinuum and partners
grants.deadlineNote: Annual; 2026 edition ran Jan 30 - Feb 1, 2026. Next edition expected late Jan / early Feb 2027.
grants.description: MIT's interdisciplinary quantum-computing hackathon with hybrid in-person and online tracks on real quantum hardware (IBM, IQM, IonQ, Quantinuum). Successor activity to the legacy IBM Quantum Open Science Prize (which has not been re-run since 2023).

grants.name: iSAFE Global Hackathon 2026 (AI for Good Summit)
grants.funder: ITU (UN) AI for Good + CyberPeace Foundation
grants.deadlineNote: AI for Good Global Summit 2026 runs Jul 7-10, 2026 in Geneva (per aiforgood.itu.int). iSAFE hackathon track not currently listed on the main summit page; applications for parallel ML challenges (TinyML, Space AI, Innovation Factory) typically close weeks before summit.
grants.description: UN ITU's flagship AI-for-Good hackathon track at the Geneva summit. Participants build technical AI solutions for deepfake detection, misinformation, environmental footprint measurement, and edge ML challenges.

grants.name: Lablab.ai AI Hackathons (rolling)
grants.funder: Lablab.ai with IBM, AMD, NVIDIA, Hugging Face, OpenAI and partners
grants.deadlineNote: Continuous calendar of online and hybrid AI hackathons; e.g. Band of Agents (Jun 12-19, 2026), SF on-site (Jun 27-28, 2026), plus monthly editions throughout 2026.
grants.description: Largest rolling calendar of AI builder hackathons globally. Topics span LLM agents, multimodal models, open-weights fine-tuning, enterprise AI, and applied ML. Open to developers and researchers worldwide.
